text: Some might think that decolonial approaches could be complementary to political discussions around the EU, in the sense that decentring approaches would then be able to lead to concrete improvements and achieve a gradual transformation of the system. But there is no guarantee that this will be the case, as long as decentring and post/decolonial agendas are confused. In fact, an effective and legitimate EU foreign policy does not necessarily mean that the coloniality of EU power will be diminished. Rather than “decentring Europe”, we need to aim at decolonial futures that serve the democratic struggle against colonial continuities.
explanation: The passage presents a nuanced, EU-specific analytic argument with carefully crafted phrasing typical of human scholarly writing. While it could be generated by AI, the subtle stance and domain-specific terminology make human authorship plausible.
